name: flowdot
description: Connect Claude to the entire FlowDot platform — 142 tools across workflows, recipes, custom nodes, apps, knowledge bases, agent toolkits, and community sharing.
long_description:
FlowDot MCP Server gives Claude a complete operational interface to the FlowDot AI workflow platform — 142 tools across 16 categories, plus 7 educational `learn://` resources.

## What Claude Can Do

- **Build workflows from scratch** — Create graphs, add nodes, wire connections, validate, execute, and stream results
- **Author custom nodes** — Write JavaScript components validated with AST parsing, then publish to the community
- **Develop FlowDot Apps** — Full multi-file React projects with surgical code editing operations
- **Design agent recipes** — Multi-step agentic programs with stores, gates, branches, loops, parallel steps, and sub-recipes
- **Manage knowledge bases (RAG)** — Categories, document upload, semantic queries
- **Create and invoke agent toolkits** — Define new tools with OAuth/API-key credentials, install, and invoke
- **Share and discover** — Public URLs, voting, comments, favorites, community browsing
- **Run and observe executions** — Start, stream via SSE, cancel, retry, view history and metrics

**Note:** Recipes can be DESIGNED through MCP but must be EXECUTED via the FlowDot CLI (`@flowdot.ai/cli`) — recipes are long-running agentic programs that exceed AI client timeouts and require local file/code/shell access.

## Educational Resources

The server exposes 7 standalone concept guides via the MCP `ReadResourceRequest` interface:
- `learn://overview`, `learn://workflows`, `learn://recipes`
- `learn://custom-nodes`, `learn://apps`, `learn://toolkits`, `learn://knowledge-base`

Read these before invoking tools to scaffold your understanding of each subsystem.

## Getting Started

1. Get your MCP token from FlowDot Settings > MCP Tokens
2. Select the scopes you need (token can be restricted to read-only or any subset)
3. Enter the token when prompted
4. Start chatting with Claude about your workflows!

api_token.title: FlowDot API Token
api_token.description: Your MCP token from FlowDot Settings > MCP Tokens. Tokens start with 'fd_mcp_'

hub_url.title: FlowDot Hub URL
hub_url.description: FlowDot Hub URL (leave default for cloud, or enter your self-hosted URL)

tools.name: list_workflows
tools.description: List all workflows accessible to the authenticated user

tools.name: execute_workflow
